preamble: Monophthongization of 'ei' to 'i' involves the simplification of the diphthong into a single high front vowel, common in Germanic languages including English.

examples.note: Historical monophthongization where diphthongs simplify to high front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
